content.text:
@CLAUDE.md Audit every Mermaid diagram across the vault, fix the broken ones, and update CLAUDE.md to prevent recurrence. Mehrmaid is now an installed plugin and may be used where it genuinely helps. Step 1 — Inventory. Find every Mermaid code block in 10-Courses/, 20-Concepts/, and 40-Reviews/. A code block is a fenced block starting with ```mermaid or ```mehrmaid. Report: Total count of mermaid blocks Total count of mehrmaid blocks (likely zero) List of files containing diagrams, grouped by folder Do not edit anything yet. Step 2 — Classify each diagram. For each Mermaid block, classify it into one of three buckets: A. Clean — valid Mermaid, renders correctly. No markdown anti-patterns inside the block. Leave alone. B. Broken syntax, structurally fine — has anti-patterns that break Mermaid parsing but the underlying diagram structure is sound. The fix is to rewrite syntax (escape, restructure, or move content out). Examples: > blockquote lines inside the block **bold**, _italic_, ==highlight== inside node labels - bullet or * bullet lines used as Mermaid statements # header lines inside the block (should be %%) Raw LaTeX $...$ inside unquoted node labels C. Genuinely needs rich markdown — the diagram's information design legitimately requires LaTeX inside node labels, tables inside nodes, internal [[wikilinks]], or multi-line formatted content. These are candidates for mehrmaid. Print a table: FileDiagram (1-based index in file)ClassIssue (if B)Why mehrmaid (if C) Be honest in classification. Default to B over C — most "needs markdown" cases are actually sloppy diagrams that should be restructured. Only classify as C when the markdown content is genuinely the point (e.g. a node whose value IS a LaTeX equation that's central to the concept). Step 3 — Fix bucket B (broken syntax, restructure to plain Mermaid). For each B diagram, rewrite as valid plain mermaid. Apply these fixes: Blockquote > notes → either move the note text out of the diagram into surrounding prose, or convert to a labeled note node connected by a dotted edge: Main -.-> Note["note text"]:::note with classDef note fill:#fff3e0,stroke:#999,stroke-dasharray:5 Markdown emphasis inside labels (**bold**, _italic_) → use Mermaid's HTML support: A["bold"], A["italic"]. If the emphasis isn't carrying meaning, just drop it. Bullet lists inside a node → split the node into multiple nodes connected by edges, OR group them in a subgraph, OR collapse into one label with
separators: A["Item 1
Item 2
Item 3"]. Prefer splitting/subgrouping when items are conceptually distinct. Headers (# Title inside a block) → use subgraph name [Title] for grouping, or move the header outside the block as the surrounding markdown header. HTML comments → replace with %% Mermaid comment (Mermaid's native comment syntax) Raw LaTeX inside a node → either move the equation to surrounding prose with the diagram referencing it ("see equation above"), or convert this specific diagram to mehrmaid (see Step 4) if the equation is the diagram's point. Apply the rewrite to the file. Show me a diff for the first three fixes before continuing to verify the approach. After I confirm, proceed through all of bucket B without further confirmation. Step 4 — Convert bucket C to mehrmaid. For each C diagram, convert from mermaid to mehrmaid: Change the fence language from ```mermaid to ```mehrmaid Wrap node content that uses markdown in ("double quoted parens") so Mehrmaid renders the markdown Keep the rest of the diagram structure identical Example transformation: mermaid: A[**Convolution**: $f * g$] mehrmaid: A("**Convolution**: $f * g$") Apply, no need to confirm individually — the C list was already vetted. Step 5 — Update CLAUDE.md. Replace the existing "Mermaid syntax constraints" subsection (if it exists) and add a "Mehrmaid for markdown-rich diagrams" subsection. The full updated diagram rules section should include: The existing "Diagrams: Mermaid first, always" mandate (unchanged) The existing image-embed allowlist (unchanged) Updated Mermaid syntax constraints (forbidden Markdown inside mermaid blocks: >, **bold**, _italic_, - bullets, # headers, , raw $LaTeX$) NEW Mehrmaid subsection: When to use mehrmaid instead of mermaid: only when node labels genuinely need LaTeX, tables, internal links, or multi-line formatted markdown Syntax: same as Mermaid, but node content wrapped in ("...") is rendered as Obsidian markdown Default to plain mermaid. Don't reach for mehrmaid to paper over bad diagram structure — restructure first Examples of legitimate mehrmaid use cases: a concept diagram where one node IS a defining equation; a flow that includes a [[wikilink]] to another concept; a node containing a small comparison table Examples of bad mehrmaid use cases: bullet lists inside a node when subgraphs would be clearer; bold/italic emphasis that doesn't add information Show me the proposed CLAUDE.md edit as a diff before applying. Step 6 — Final report. When done, print: BucketCountFiles affectedA — clean, untouchedB — broken, fixed to plain mermaidC — converted to mehrmaid Also list any diagrams you weren't sure how to classify and why. I'll resolve those manually. Hard rules throughout: Never silently delete content. If a diagram has prose annotations that don't fit cleanly anywhere, move them to surrounding markdown around the diagram, not into the void. Never invent diagram content. If you can't tell what a broken diagram was trying to show, flag it for me to handle, don't guess. Preserve all surrounding markdown (headers, prose, frontmatter) exactly. Only the diagram blocks should change. Test render after fixing if possible — if Mermaid throws a syntax error after your rewrite, the fix is wrong; iterate. Start with Step 1.

content.text: Let me scan for subtler Mermaid syntax issues — unquoted parens, brackets, or other characters that often break parsing.

content.title:
for f in $(find 10-Courses 20-Concepts -name "*.md" 2>/dev/null); do awk -v file="$f" 'BEGIN{inblock=0; bidx=0} /^```mermaid/{inblock=1; bidx++; next} /^```$/ && inblock{inblock=0; next} inblock{
  # Look for square-bracket node defs containing unquoted special chars
  # Pattern like NodeID[Text with ( or ) or , without quotes]
  if (match($0, /[A-Za-z0-9_]+\[[^"][^]]*[(),][^]]*\]/)) print file " | block " bidx " | UNQUOTED-SPECIAL-IN-[]: " $0
  # Curly decision nodes with potentially problematic content
  if (match($0, /[A-Za-z0-9_]+\{[^"][^}]*<br/) && !match($0, /\{"/)) print file " | block " bidx " | UNQUOTED-BR-IN-{}: " $0
  # Unquoted parens in stadium nodes
  if (match($0, /[A-Za-z0-9_]+\(\([^"][^)]*[(),][^)]*\)\)/)) print file " | block " bidx " | UNQUOTED-SPECIAL-IN-(()): " $0
}' "$f"; done

for f in $(find 10-Courses 20-Concepts -name "*.md" 2>/dev/null); do awk -v file="$f" 'BEGIN{inblock=0; bidx=0} /^```mermaid/{inblock=1; bidx++; next} /^```$/ && inblock{inblock=0; next} inblock{
  # Look for square-bracket node defs containing unquoted special chars
  # Pattern like NodeID[Text with ( or ) or , without quotes]
  if (match($0, /[A-Za-z0-9_]+\[[^"][^]]*[(),][^]]*\]/)) print file " | block " bidx " | UNQUOTED-SPECIAL-IN-[]: " $0
  # Curly decision nodes with potentially problematic content
  if (match($0, /[A-Za-z0-9_]+\{[^"][^}]*<br/) && !match($0, /\{"/)) print file " | block " bidx " | UNQUOTED-BR-IN-{}: " $0
  # Unquoted parens in stadium nodes
  if (match($0, /[A-Za-z0-9_]+\(\([^"][^)]*[(),][^)]*\)\)/)) print file " | block " bidx " | UNQUOTED-SPECIAL-IN-(()): " $0
}' "$f"; done

content.text: Found more files I missed. And `mmdc` is available — I can actually test-render every diagram. Let me extract and validate all 78 blocks programmatically.

content.title:
mkdir -p /tmp/mermaid-audit && cd /tmp/mermaid-audit && rm -rf blocks && mkdir -p blocks
cd /Users/mattporteous/Obsidian/School
python3 <<'PY'
import os, re, json, pathlib
root = pathlib.Path("/Users/mattporteous/Obsidian/School")
folders = ["10-Courses", "20-Concepts", "40-Reviews"]
results = []
idx = 0
for folder in folders:
    for md in sorted((root / folder).rglob("*.md")):
        text = md.read_text()
        # Match fenced mermaid blocks (allow leading whitespace)
        # Group: leading whitespace
        pattern = re.compile(r"^([ \t]*)```mermaid[ \t]*\r?\n(.*?)^\1```[ \t]*$", re.MULTILINE | re.DOTALL)
        for i, m in enumerate(pattern.finditer(text), start=1):
            content = m.group(2)
            # de-indent
            indent = m.group(1)
            if indent:
                lines = content.split("\n")
                stripped = []
                for L in lines:
                    if L.startswith(indent):
                        stripped.append(L[len(indent):])
                    else:
                        stripped.append(L)
                content = "\n".join(stripped)
            idx += 1
            outpath = pathlib.Path(f"/tmp/mermaid-audit/blocks/{idx:03d}.mmd")
            outpath.write_text(content)
            results.append({
                "id": idx,
                "file": str(md.relative_to(root)),
                "block": i,
                "path": str(outpath),
            })
pathlib.Path("/tmp/mermaid-audit/index.json").write_text(json.dumps(results, indent=2))
print(f"Extracted {idx} blocks")
PY

        for i, m in enumerate(pattern.finditer(text), start=1):
            content = m.group(2)

rawInput.description: Extract all mermaid blocks to individual files

content.title:
cd /tmp/mermaid-audit && mkdir -p out && cat > render.sh <<'BASH'
#!/bin/bash
fail=0
ok=0
for f in blocks/*.mmd; do
  base=$(basename "$f" .mmd)
  if mmdc -i "$f" -o "out/$base.svg" --quiet 2>"out/$base.err" >/dev/null; then
    ok=$((ok+1))
  else
    fail=$((fail+1))
    echo "FAIL: $base"
  fi
done
echo "OK: $ok  FAIL: $fail"
BASH
chmod +x render.sh
./render.sh 2>&1 | tail -100

content.text:
## Step 2 — Classification I extracted all 79 mermaid blocks (the count grew from 63 to 79 because newer files included indented blocks I missed in the first pass), then validated each one two ways: (a) Mermaid parser via the locally-installed `mermaid` package + JSDOM, and (b) full SVG render via `mmdc` with headless Chrome. **Result: 79/79 parse and render successfully.** I also scanned every block for the specific markdown anti-patterns you listed (`>` blockquote, `**bold**`, `_italic_`, `==highlight==`, raw `-`/`*` bullet statements, `#` headers, `` comments, raw `$LaTeX$` outside quoted labels) — zero hits. | File | # Diagrams | Class | Notes | |---|---|---|---| | 10-Courses/Computer-Vision/lectures/lecture-10-fourier-transform-2.md | 5 | A | clean | | 10-Courses/Computer-Vision/lectures/lecture-11-feature-detectors.md | 8 | A | clean | | 10-Courses/Computer-Vision/lectures/lecture-12-feature-detectors-2.md | 6 | A | clean | | 10-Courses/Computer-Vision/lectures/lecture-13-binary-object-characterization.md | 5 | A | clean | | 10-Courses/Computer-Vision/lectures/lecture-14-intro-to-pytorch.md | 7 | A | clean | | 10-Courses/Computer-Vision/lectures/lecture-15-intro-to-dl.md | 9 | A | clean | | 10-Courses/Computer-Vision/lectures/lecture-16-cnns.md | 7 | A | clean | | 10-Courses/Computer-Vision/lectures/lecture-17-advanced-architectures.md | 12 | A | clean | | 20-Concepts/activation-functions.md | 1 | A | clean | | 20-Concepts/backpropagation.md | 1 | A | clean | | 20-Concepts/cnn-architecture.md | 1 | A | clean | | 20-Concepts/cv-tasks.md | 1 | A | clean | | 20-Concepts/data-augmentation.md | 1 | A | clean | | 20-Concepts/depthwise-separable-conv.md | 1 | A | clean | | 20-Concepts/difference-of-gaussian.md | 1 | A | clean | | 20-Concepts/feature-map.md | 1 | A | clean | | 20-Concepts/inception-module.md | 2 | A | clean | | 20-Concepts/inductive-bias.md | 1 | A | clean | | 20-Concepts/loss-functions.md | 1 | A | clean | | 20-Concepts/mini-batch-sgd.md | 1 | A | clean | | 20-Concepts/pooling.md | 1 | A | clean | | 20-Concepts/pytorch-dataloader.md | 1 | A | clean | | 20-Concepts/receptive-field.md | 1 | A | clean | | 20-Concepts/residual-connection.md | 1 | A | clean | | 20-Concepts/vanishing-exploding-gradients.md | 1 | A | clean | | 20-Concepts/vggnet.md | 1 | A | clean | | **TOTAL** | **79** | **all A** | | **Bucket B (broken syntax): 0** **Bucket C (genuinely needs mehrmaid): 0** — there are some diagrams where equations appear in node labels (e.g. lecture-15 backprop walk, lecture-11 Hough sinusoid), but they currently render as plain text with `
`, and your guidance is "default to B over C; only convert when markdown content IS the point." The current rendering works, so I'm leaving them. So **Steps 3 and 4 are no-ops.** Diagrams I was unsure how to classify: none — the audit was unambiguous. Want me to push further on any specific diagrams you suspect are broken in Obsidian's renderer specifically? Otherwise, on to Step 5.## Step 5 — Proposed CLAUDE.md edit (diff before applying) I'd insert two new subsections inside the existing "Diagrams: Mermaid first, always" section, between the existing "Forbidden" and "Mermaid quality bar" subsections. Existing rules (mandate, image allowlist, image embed format, where diagrams go, mermaid rendering quality) stay as-is. ```diff @@ after the existing "Forbidden" subsection @@ +### Mermaid syntax constraints + +A `​`​`​`mermaid block is a diagramming DSL, not a Markdown context. The following Markdown is FORBIDDEN inside the block — it either breaks the parser or embeds noise that doesn't render: + +- `>` blockquote lines (Mermaid mis-parses) +- `**bold**`, `_italic_`, `==highlight==` inside node labels — use Mermaid's HTML support: `A["bold"]`, `A["italic"]`. If the emphasis isn't carrying meaning, drop it. +- `-` or `*` bullet lines used as Mermaid statements — split into multiple nodes, group in a `subgraph`, or collapse into one label with `
` separators. +- `#` header lines inside the block — use `subgraph Name [Display Label]` for grouping, or move the header outside the diagram. +- `` — use `%% Mermaid comment` instead. +- Raw `$...$` LaTeX inside unquoted node labels — either move the equation to surrounding prose ("see equation above") or convert that one diagram to mehrmaid (see below). + +Always quote node labels that contain spaces, punctuation, parens, or `
`: `A["text with, comma"]`, not `A[text with, comma]`. Decision nodes too: `q1{"Question?"}`, not `q1{Question?}`. + +If a diagram seems to need richer formatting than these rules permit, the answer is to restructure the diagram, not to paper over with Markdown. Annotations, prose notes, equations that don't fit cleanly belong in the surrounding Markdown around the block. + +### Mehrmaid for markdown-rich diagrams + +Mehrmaid is an installed Obsidian plugin that extends Mermaid: any double-quoted text in the diagram is rendered as Obsidian Markdown — so LaTeX, internal `[[wikilinks]]`, and small tables work inside nodes. By convention, signal markdown intent by wrapping node content in `("...")` (rounded-rect with quotes). + +**When to use `​`​`​`mehrmaid instead of `​`​`​`mermaid:** + +- A node's value IS a defining LaTeX equation, central to the concept. +- The diagram links to another concept note via `[[wikilink]]`. +- A single node legitimately holds a small comparison table. + +**When NOT to use mehrmaid (default to plain mermaid):** + +- Bullet lists inside a node — split into multiple nodes or a `subgraph` instead. +- `**bold**` / `_italic_` emphasis that doesn't add information — drop it. +- "It would look nicer" / "the slide had pretty math." Restructure the diagram first. + +**Syntax:** identical to Mermaid, except the fence language is `mehrmaid` and markdown-rendered nodes wrap content in `("...")`. Example transformation: + +``` +mermaid: A[Convolution: f * g] +mehrmaid: A("**Convolution**: $f * g$") +``` + +Don't reach for mehrmaid to paper over a sloppy diagram; it's a tool for the small set of cases where Markdown content IS the point. + @@ existing "Mermaid quality bar" subsection follows unchanged @@ ``` That's the only addition — no other CLAUDE.md content changes. Apply it?

entries.content: Step 3 — Fix bucket B diagrams (no-op, B is empty)
Step 4 — Convert bucket C to mehrmaid (no-op, C is empty)
